architecture_summary: Build the Phase 1 capability as a bounded extraction and export path governed by the Agent PRD contract.
component_boundaries: Keep transcript input, evidence-backed extraction, human confirmation, and Markdown export as explicit component boundaries.
data_and_state_design: Treat authorized transcript text as input, extracted action items as review state, and confirmed Markdown as the only output.
integration_points: No external task-system integration is allowed in Phase 1; Markdown export is the integration boundary.
verification_strategy: Use the contract verification refs and acceptance criteria to prove extraction, confirmation, stop, and export behavior.
risk_controls: Stop when authorization is missing or a request attempts automatic external task creation.

control_flow_design.name: Accept authorized transcript input
control_flow_design.trigger: A reviewer provides authorized transcript text for Phase 1 extraction.
control_flow_design.failure_or_branch_behavior: If authorization is missing or the request asks for external task creation, stop under STOP-001.

control_flow_design.name: Extract and confirm action items
control_flow_design.failure_or_branch_behavior: Items without source evidence remain unexported; confirmed items are rendered to Markdown only after human confirmation.

data_flow_design.transformation: Extract action item, owner, due date when present, and source evidence under EXE-001.
data_flow_design.target: Review state for human confirmation and confirmed Markdown output.
data_flow_design.persistence_or_retention: Only confirmed Markdown is an output artifact; unconfirmed extraction state is not an accepted deliverable.

data_objects.purpose: Represents an extracted action item awaiting human confirmation.

fields.name: action
fields.meaning: The follow-up action extracted from transcript text.

fields.name: owner
fields.meaning: The responsible person when present in the transcript.

fields.name: due_date
fields.meaning: The due date when present in the transcript.

fields.name: source_evidence
fields.meaning: Transcript evidence supporting the candidate item.

fields.name: confirmation_status
fields.meaning: Human confirmation state before Markdown export.

data_objects.lifecycle: Created from authorized transcript text, reviewed by a human, and exported only after confirmation.

interface_contracts.invocation: Provide authorized transcript text as the Phase 1 input boundary.
interface_contracts.error_semantics: Reject missing authorization and requests for automatic external task creation.

interface_contracts.invocation: Export confirmed action items after confirmation.
outputs: Confirmed Markdown action-item list from OUT-001

transitions: to extraction when authorization is present
to stopped when authorization is missing or external task creation is requested
to Markdown export after human confirmation
remain unexported when confirmation is absent

technical_decisions.decision: Keep Phase 1 export limited to Markdown.
technical_decisions.rationale: OOS-001 excludes automatic Jira, Linear, Asana, calendar, or email task creation.
technical_decisions.implementation_notes: Implement external task-system creation as unavailable in Phase 1 and guard it through STOP-001.

technical_decisions.decision: Require source evidence for every exported action item.
technical_decisions.rationale: REQ-001 and AC-001 require source evidence and human confirmation.
technical_decisions.implementation_notes: Carry source_evidence through the candidate object and block export if evidence is absent.

implementation_design.design: Extract only evidence-backed candidates from authorized transcript text and preserve enough state for human confirmation.
Generate Markdown only from confirmed candidates and keep external task-system creation out of Phase 1.

environment_requirements.requirement: Run acceptance on the actual Phase 1 extractor build with Markdown export enabled.
environment_requirements.required_for: Proving AC-001, VER-001, OUT-001, STOP-001, and DONE-001.

real_acceptance_plan.environment: Confirmed local Phase 1 extractor runtime at C:/tmp/spec-intake-acceptance/meeting-action-extractor with Markdown export enabled and external task-system connectors disabled.
preconditions: The real extractor runtime exists at C:/tmp/spec-intake-acceptance/meeting-action-extractor.
The authorized transcript file exists at C:/tmp/spec-intake-acceptance/data/authorized-meeting-transcript-001.txt.
External task-system connectors are disabled for Phase 1 acceptance.
execution_steps: Run the actual extractor with the authorized transcript text from IN-001.
Confirm the extracted item through the human confirmation boundary required by AC-001.
Export the confirmed Markdown action-item list from OUT-001.
Attempt an external task-creation request and verify STOP-001 prevents it.
expected_results: The result contains action, owner when present, due date when present, and source evidence.
Markdown export occurs only after human confirmation.
External task-system creation remains unavailable in Phase 1.
mechanical_checks: Verify actions.md exists and contains only confirmed action items.
Verify extraction-run.json links each exported action to source evidence.
Verify stop-condition-external-task.json records refusal of external task creation.
failure_criteria: Fail if Markdown export occurs before human confirmation.
Fail if any exported action item lacks source evidence.
Fail if external task creation is attempted or succeeds.
Fail if required evidence artifacts are missing.
evidence_to_capture: Input transcript identifier or acceptance fixture path approved for real acceptance.
Extractor run output before and after human confirmation.
Stop-condition evidence for attempted external task creation.

design_gate_report.name: HLD source readiness
design_gate_report.evidence_summary: The HLD is derived from approved PRD and execution-ready Agent PRD contract refs.

design_gate_report.name: Control flow readiness
design_gate_report.evidence_summary: The HLD defines ordered triggers, components, inputs, outputs, interactions, and branch or failure behavior for the current phase.

design_gate_report.name: Data flow and object readiness
design_gate_report.evidence_summary: The HLD defines data source, transformation, target, retention, data object fields, field meanings, required status, lifecycle, and contract refs.

design_gate_report.name: Interface and state readiness
design_gate_report.evidence_summary: The HLD defines provider, consumer, invocation, inputs, outputs, error semantics, state owners, and state transitions.

design_gate_report.name: Technical implementation readiness
design_gate_report.evidence_summary: The HLD records implementation decisions, rationale, implementation notes, risk controls, and component-level implementation design.

design_gate_report.name: Real acceptance readiness
design_gate_report.evidence_summary: The HLD names concrete confirmed real environment, real data, execution steps, expected results, evidence capture, acceptance owner, and no-mock policy.

design_gate_report.name: HLD document readiness
design_gate_report.evidence_summary: The Markdown HLD renders source-backed interface precision and executable acceptance design with command, preconditions, expected artifacts, mechanical checks, and failure criteria.

design_gate_report.name: Task boundary readiness
design_gate_report.evidence_summary: The HLD remains an implementation design and contains no task plan, task graph, dependency edge, parallel group, or implementation task card.
